# Equihash solver known-answer test vectors
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Read at startup by the equihash self-test (algo/equihash/equihash-gate.c).
# Each line validates equihash_verify() against a REAL (block header, solution)
# pair from the chain. A PASS proves the Blake2b personalization, hash
# generation, ExpandArray and index packing match the canonical definition.
#
# Lines starting with '#' and blank lines are ignored.
#
# Two accepted formats (whitespace-separated):
#
#   <wn> <wk> <personal8> <rawblock_hex>
#   <wn> <wk> <personal8> <header140_hex> <solution_hex>
#
#   wn,wk        : equihash parameters, e.g. 200 9  /  144 5  /  96 5
#   personal8    : 8-char personalization, e.g. ZcashPoW / BitcoinZ / ZelProoW
#   rawblock_hex : the serialized block. Only the leading header(140 B) +
#                  compactSize + solution bytes are read; trailing transaction
#                  data is ignored, so pasting just the first ~3000 hex chars is
#                  enough. Slicing from the raw block avoids byte-order mistakes.
#   header140_hex/solution_hex : alternative explicit form (280 hex header +
#                  the raw solution hex WITHOUT the compactSize prefix).
#
# How to obtain a raw block (gives the safe rawblock_hex form):
#
#   zcash-cli    getblock <blockhash> 0      # Zcash 200/9, personal "ZcashPoW"
#   bitcoinz-cli getblock <blockhash> 0      # BitcoinZ 144/5, personal "BitcoinZ"
#   flux/zel:    getblock via daemon         # 125/4, personal "ZelProoW"
#
# or any block explorer's "raw"/"hex" view for the block.
#
# Example (REPLACE with a real block — this placeholder is intentionally
# commented out so it does not produce a false FAIL):
#
# 144 5 BitcoinZ 04000000....<rawblockhex>....
# 200 9 ZcashPoW 04000000....<rawblockhex>....
144 5 BitcoinZ
